# UC-12.2.2 · Build Duration Monitoring

## Description

Increasing build times slow development velocity. Detection enables build optimization and infrastructure right-sizing.

## Value

Increasing build times slow development velocity. Detection enables build optimization and infrastructure right-sizing.

## Implementation

Track build duration for all pipelines. Alert when duration exceeds historical average by >50%. Identify slow build steps. Correlate with infrastructure metrics (runner CPU, disk I/O) to find bottlenecks.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk App for Jenkins, CI/CD metrics.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Build start/end timestamps.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Track build duration for all pipelines. Alert when duration exceeds historical average by >50%. Identify slow build steps. Correlate with infrastructure metrics (runner CPU, disk I/O) to find bottlenecks.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cicd sourcetype="jenkins:build" result="SUCCESS"
| eval duration_min=round(duration/60000,1)
| timechart span=1d avg(duration_min) as avg_build_time by job_name
```

Understanding this SPL

**Build Duration Monitoring** — Increasing build times slow development velocity. Detection enables build optimization and infrastructure right-sizing.

Documented **Data sources**: Build start/end timestamps. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk App for Jenkins, CI/CD metrics. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cicd; **sourcetype**: jenkins:build. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=cicd, sourcetype="jenkins:build". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **duration_min** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=1d** buckets with a separate series **by job_name** — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with Jenkins build history. Verify field extractions and index permissions.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (build duration trend), Bar chart (avg duration by pipeline), Table (slowest builds today).

## SPL

```spl
index=cicd sourcetype="jenkins:build" result="SUCCESS"
| eval duration_min=round(duration/60000,1)
| timechart span=1d avg(duration_min) as avg_build_time by job_name
```

## Visualization

Line chart (build duration trend), Bar chart (avg duration by pipeline), Table (slowest builds today).
